mission.title: Install and Configure Carvel on Kubernetes
mission.description: Ytt is a YAML templating tool that allows you to manage complex configurations using reusable templates and structured YAML. Installing ytt helps streamline your configuration management process, making it easier to handle environment-specific values and custom validations.

steps.title: Add the Carvel Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Carvel Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the ytt chart.
```bash
helm repo add carvel https://charts.carvel.dev
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install ytt using Helm
steps.description:
Install ytt in the 'carvel' namespace using Helm. This command will create the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install ytt carvel/ytt --namespace carvel --create-namespace --version v0.53.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the ytt pod is running successfully in the 'carvel' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace carvel
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for ytt
steps.description:
Update the ytt deployment to set resource limits. This step ensures that ytt has the necessary resources allocated.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/ytt --namespace carvel --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for local access
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access ytt locally. This allows you to interact with ytt through your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/ytt --namespace carvel 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the ytt pod running in the 'carvel' namespace, and you will be able to access it locally through port 8080. You can also verify that resource limits are set correctly for the deployment.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add carvel https://charts.carvel.dev
helm repo update
helm install ytt carvel/ytt --namespace carvel --create-namespace --version v0.53.2

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed.
